reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD as safe due to pinned GitHub sources, verified checksums, and standard binary installation without dangerous commands or dynamic execution risks.
coverage_issues: Both models failed to review potential .install hooks or other package scripts. The tool should prioritize reviewing all executable scripts referenced in PKGBUILD.
false_negatives: Potential runtime risks (network access by the binary) were noted but correctly deemed out-of-scope for AUR audits.
learnings: File selection must prioritize all executable package scripts (e.g., .install hooks) regardless of model discretion
Add explicit checks for hook scripts during initial file selection
Improve model guidance to distinguish between build-time risks (in-scope) and runtime behavior (out-of-scope)
Implement mandatory review of any file referenced in PKGBUILD functions (package(), prepare(), etc.)
